meaning: adj.兴奋的,热切的adv.热切地,渴望
examples: The children were all agog to hear the story.
She arrived, agog, to discover what had prevented him being there.
The beauty of these towns, particularly Trogir and Dubrovnik, left us agog.
He has to say something on the "peace process" and we all wait agog for any news-making pronouncement.
The world is looking on, agog, and wondering why the United States seems intent on jumping off this cliff.
